summary: Development of a comprehensive food delivery platform facilitating the complete order lifecycle.
The project involves creating a multi-faceted ecosystem including interfaces for customers, restaurants, and administrators.
explicit_requirements: Centralized administrative dashboard for platform management
assumptions: The system will require three distinct front-end interfaces: Customer App, Restaurant App/Portal, and a Delivery Driver App.
Live tracking will utilize GPS-based mapping services (e.g., Google Maps API).
Payments will be handled via third-party providers (e.g., Stripe, PayPal).
The application needs to be cross-platform (iOS and Android).
missing_information: Specific geographic target markets and localization needs.
Preferred tech stack or existing infrastructure constraints.
Delivery fleet management details (In-house drivers vs. third-party logistics).
Budget, timeline, and MVP (Minimum Viable Product) priorities.
Compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR, PCI-DSS for payments).

modules.description: Centralized authentication and authorization service for customers, restaurant owners, delivery drivers, and administrators.
features: Profile management for all user types

modules.description: Handles restaurant onboarding, menu configuration, and operational status management.
The core engine managing the order lifecycle from cart creation to delivery completion.
Integration with third-party providers to handle secure transactions, refunds, and payouts.
Coordinates delivery personnel and provides live GPS tracking data to all stakeholders.
features: Live GPS tracking via Google Maps API

modules.description: A feedback loop for users to rate experiences and for the platform to maintain quality control.
A web-based dashboard for platform owners to monitor performance and manage global settings.
Cross-platform messaging service to keep users informed about order updates and promotions.

summary: The client intends to develop a comprehensive Hospital Management System (HMS) to digitize patient health records and medical workflows.
Key focus areas include administrative scheduling, financial billing, and high-level data security compliant with international healthcare standards.
assumptions: The application will require role-based access control (RBAC) for doctors, nurses, and administrative staff.
A centralized database will be used to store Sensitive Personal Information (SPI).
The billing module will likely need to support insurance claims or standard payment processing.
The 'HIPAA style' requirement implies data encryption, audit trails, and secure user authentication.
missing_information: Technical platform preference (Web, Mobile, or Cross-platform).
Expected scale of the application (Single clinic versus a multi-specialty hospital chain).
Integration needs for third-party services (e.g., Pharmacy, Laboratory Information Systems, or Payment Gateways).
Specific language or localization requirements for the user interface.
